description:
You are a FinOps analyst reviewing the preliminary cost data for Q2-2023.  
    A colleague has handed you a ready-made unified diff that corrects several line items and appends one new service row.  
    Your job is to apply that patch and capture proof that it was applied cleanly.

    Initial workspace  
    • Directory: /home/user/finops_reports  
      – /home/user/finops_reports/cost_report_Q2_2023.csv (the base cost report)  
      – /home/user/finops_reports/cost_report_Q2_2023.patch (the unified diff you must apply)

    What you must do  
    1. Apply the patch file to the CSV so that the original file is modified in-place.  
       • Do NOT create a separate “.rej” or backup copy; the original must be overwritten with the new, optimized values.  
    2. Redirect the stdout produced by the patch program to the log file  
       /home/user/finops_reports/patch_apply.log  
       – The log will be used for automated verification, so capture *only* the program’s normal stdout, not stderr.  
    3. Once the patch succeeds, display the full, updated /home/user/finops_reports/cost_report_Q2_2023.csv to the terminal so a human reviewer can see the optimized numbers.

    Very important output-format requirements  
    • /home/user/finops_reports/patch_apply.log must exist and contain at least the exact line  
      patching file cost_report_Q2_2023.csv  
      (nothing else is forbidden, but that line must appear verbatim).  
    • /home/user/finops_reports/cost_report_Q2_2023.csv must match the patched content byte-for-byte; automated tests will diff it against an answer key.  

    You may inspect the patch file before applying it, but the **only** file you should modify is  
    /home/user/finops_reports/cost_report_Q2_2023.csv.  

    Hint: this can be accomplished with a single shell command that both applies the patch and redirects stdout to the required log file.  No super-user privileges are needed.
truth:
Existing files before the agent begins:

    1. /home/user/finops_reports/cost_report_Q2_2023.csv  
       Contents (including the trailing newline on every line):  
       Project,Service,Month,CostUSD
       ProjectA,Compute,2023-04,1500.00
       ProjectA,Storage,2023-04,200.00
       ProjectB,Compute,2023-04,1800.00
       ProjectB,Database,2023-04,600.00

    2. /home/user/finops_reports/cost_report_Q2_2023.patch  
       Exact contents:  
       --- cost_report_Q2_2023.csv	2023-05-01 12:00:00.000000000 +0000
       +++ cost_report_Q2_2023.csv	2023-05-02 12:00:00.000000000 +0000
       @@ -1,5 +1,6 @@
        Project,Service,Month,CostUSD
        ProjectA,Compute,2023-04,1500.00
-       ProjectA,Storage,2023-04,200.00
+       ProjectA,Storage,2023-04,150.00
        ProjectB,Compute,2023-04,1800.00
-       ProjectB,Database,2023-04,600.00
+       ProjectB,Database,2023-04,450.00
+       ProjectC,Analytics,2023-04,300.00

    Desired final state after the agent completes the task:

    a. /home/user/finops_reports/cost_report_Q2_2023.csv  
       Contents must be exactly:  
       Project,Service,Month,CostUSD
       ProjectA,Compute,2023-04,1500.00
       ProjectA,Storage,2023-04,150.00
       ProjectB,Compute,2023-04,1800.00
       ProjectB,Database,2023-04,450.00
       ProjectC,Analytics,2023-04,300.00

    b. /home/user/finops_reports/patch_apply.log  
       Must exist and contain the literal substring:  
       patching file cost_report_Q2_2023.csv  
       (Automated tests will grep for that exact string.)

    No other files should be created or modified.
